original_abstract:
The development of a national legal system requires standardized methods, materials, and binding frameworks to guide authorized institutions in the legislative process. In Indonesia, Law No. 12 of 2011 on the Formation of Legislation serves as the primary manual for creating legal instruments. However, since its implementation, this Act has encountered significant legal challenges at both the normative and practical levels. These issues include ambiguities regarding the judicial review of MPR Decrees, the lack of clarity concerning the hierarchy of regulations issued by state institutions, and inconsistencies in the integration of Ministerial Regulations and local governance autonomy. This study identifies these problematic gaps and argues for legislative reform or the introduction of new implementing regulations to ensure legal certainty and strengthen the integrity of the Indonesian legislative hierarchy.

**Keywords:** development of national law, formation of legislation, legal problems
humanized_abstract:
There must be set of institutional mechanisms, legislative tools and legal rules that are enforced on all legislating institutions that must work within legislative frameworks. One of the legislative tools in Indonesia used for making statutes or legislative instruments is Law No. 12 of 2011 on Legislation. Unfortunately since it is implemented, there have been a considerable amount of legal controversies which are stemmed from normative as well as pragmatic approach of the laws. Thus the study aims at among others to identify the legislation gaps in Indonesian Legislation system, as well as for dealing with the occurrence of legal ambiguities and ambiguities that arose as a result of enforcement of The Law No. 12 of 2011 concerning Legislative. By implementing legislative reforms, legislative instruments which function as the regulatory base for legislation process must be supported with amendments to rules in legislation so as to uphold legal certainty and maintain the integrity of legislative framework of the state.

**Keywords:** development of national law, formation of legislation, legal problems
